trajectory_summary: Melanie arrived at peak motivation — prior blocker resolved, explicitly ready to book — and Randy converted that into a calendar entry without building any structural integrity around the appointment. The call shape is energy_leaked: the customer supplied all the forward momentum, and the rep's handling reduced the probability of a held sit rather than protecting it. The booking exists on paper; the show-rate predictors (one-hour block stated, phone/computer anchor, 'is that fair' handshake) are absent, and the financing structure Melanie described was validated without verification. A 45-second calendar hold at 01:42–02:27 with no bridging talk further bled the call's energy. The text-followup promise at 02:44 is the one button-up element Randy completed.
outcome_dispute: The call produced a booked appointment, but the booking is structurally fragile. All show-rate-predictive button-up elements per button_up.required_elements — one_hour_block_stated, no_driving_at_computer, is_that_fair_handshake — were skipped. The text-followup promise was made (02:44), which is the sole completed element. The v1 tag of 'unknown' is consistent with the transcript, but if the system tags this as a clean sit, that would be an overstatement: this appointment carries meaningful no-show risk.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Button-up protocol completion on warm/returning customers
primary_coaching_focus.quote: I'll just send you a confirmation text with the date and time of the meeting.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Randy completed one of five required button-up elements on a customer who arrived fully motivated and offered zero resistance. The omissions — one-hour block, phone/computer anchor, 'is that fair' handshake — are not explained by call difficulty; they reflect a habit gap. This is the highest-leverage coaching target because the fix is mechanical (a checklist), the customer was maximally cooperative, and the no-show risk is entirely self-inflicted. If Randy can't complete the button-up on Melanie, he won't complete it on harder calls.

key_moments.quote: I'm ready to move forward with whatever the next step is.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the call's highest-value moment. Melanie has self-resolved her prior blocker, volunteered the resolution unprompted, and explicitly handed Randy a green light. A rep operating strategically would anchor on this — 'That's exactly what we needed to hear, Melanie — let me make sure we get you in front of the right specialist' — and use it to build appointment commitment before moving to scheduling mechanics. Randy moves directly to the calendar without acknowledging the significance of what Melanie just said.

key_moments.quote: This was gonna be a program where I I would be you you would be the owner or you would rent it out to somebody, and I didn't have to pay for anything. And then I buy the the energy at a set price.
key_moments.stewart_read: Melanie is describing what sounds like a PPA or lease structure — 'you would be the owner,' 'I didn't have to pay for anything,' 'I buy the energy at a set price.' Randy responds 'Yeah. Sounds like what we're still offering' without verifying whether Ion currently offers this structure or whether Melanie's memory of the program is accurate. The schema does not confirm Ion's PPA/lease availability (TBD area). If Ion's current offering differs from what Melanie remembers, the closer walks into a product-expectation mismatch that Randy has now implicitly endorsed. This is a knowledge gap compounded by a scope-discipline failure: Randy is confirming product structure details that belong to the closer's conversation.

key_moments.quote: So anytime, like, aft can we say anytime, like, between three and five?
key_moments.stewart_read: Melanie gives Randy a two-hour window. Randy goes silent for 45 seconds (01:42–02:27) to check the calendar. There is no bridging talk during this hold — no rapport, no appointment-framing, no 'while I pull that up, let me tell you what to expect on the call.' The hold itself is operationally necessary, but the silence is a pure energy drain on a customer who arrived warm. The call's momentum, already not being built on, is actively bled here.

key_moments.quote: I'll just send you a confirmation text with the date and time of the meeting.
key_moments.stewart_read: Randy completes exactly one of the five required button-up elements: the text-followup promise. He skips one_hour_block_stated, phone_appointment_specified, no_driving_at_computer, and is_that_fair_handshake. Each of these omissions independently raises no-show risk. The 'is that fair' handshake is the verbal commitment that converts a calendar entry into a kept appointment — its absence is the single highest-risk gap. The booking is real but fragile: Melanie agreed to a time, not to a commitment.
